findings.evidence: `Product.lean:249` defines `ComoduleCat.prod`, and `Product.lean:256-269` only defines the four canonical maps; grep of the new file shows no `lift`, `IsLimit`, `HasBinaryProduct`, `biprod`, or universal-property declaration.
findings.fix: Add the characteristic API: a `prodLift`/`prodMap` or `BinaryFan.IsLimit`/`HasBinaryProduct` characterization, plus simp lemmas for its projections; if the intended object is additive, add the biproduct identities for `prodInl`, `prodInr`, `prodFst`, and `prodSnd`.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.prod` is exposed with projections/inclusions but no introduction/elimination or universal-property API, so downstream code cannot use it as a product/biproduct without rebuilding morphisms and ext proofs by hand.

findings.evidence: `Product.lean:177-181` marks `def Prod : Comodule R C (M × N)` as `@[reducible]`, while `Product.lean:183-189` immediately provides the coaction characterization lemma.
findings.fix: Remove `@[reducible]`; keep clients using `Prod_coact`, `prodCoact_apply`, `prodCoact_inl`, and `prodCoact_inr`, adding any missing simp/toLinearMap lemmas instead of relying on reducibility.
findings.issue: `Comodule.Prod` is marked `@[reducible]`, exposing the structure body as part of the public interface even though `Prod_coact` and `prodCoact_*` are already the intended characteristic lemmas.

summary: The construction exposes the product object and canonical maps, but the public API does not yet characterize it as the categorical product/direct sum needed for additive comodule infrastructure. It also makes the comodule structure reducible instead of relying on the supplied characteristic lemmas.
